entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A major warm-season nutricereal crop grown globally, particularly in arid and semi-arid tropical regions

entities.name: ARID AND SEMI-ARID TROPICAL REGIONS
entities.description: Climatic regions where over 95% of pearl millet is grown

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: A continent where approximately 11 million hectares of pearl millet are grown

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: A continent where approximately 22 million hectares of pearl millet are grown

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The largest producer of pearl millet with an average production of 8.61 million tonnes

entities.name: CROSS-POLLINATION
entities.description: A biological process in pearl millet with an outcrossing rate of more than 85%

entities.name: PROTOGYNOUS FLOWERING
entities.description: A trait in pearl millet that favors cross-pollination

entities.name: WIND-BORNE POLLINATION
entities.description: A process that favors cross-pollination in pearl millet

entities.name: OPEN-POLLINATED VARIETIES
entities.description: The natural cultivar state of pearl millet, which are highly heterogeneous and morphologically variable

entities.name: HETEROZYGOSITY
entities.description: A genetic state that is more achievable in single-cross hybrids than in open-pollinated varieties

entities.name: HETEROSIS
entities.description: The hybrid vigor that is more achievable in single-cross hybrids than in open-pollinated varieties

entities.name: SINGLE-CROSS HYBRIDS
entities.description: Hybrids that generally provide 20-30% more grain yield than open-pollinated varieties

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: A trait that is significantly higher in single-cross hybrids compared to open-pollinated varieties

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC-NUCLEAR MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A commercially exploitable trait used for grain hybrid development

entities.name: GRAIN HYBRID DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: A method embarked upon by NARS in India for pearl millet improvement

entities.name: HYBRID BREEDING
entities.description: A method toward which most pearl millet research in India is currently directed

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: An organization playing a pivotal role in developing improved breeding lines and potential hybrid parents for pearl millet

entities.name: NARS
entities.description: The National Agricultural Research System in India involved in grain hybrid development and testing

entities.name: PATANCHERU
entities.description: The location where ICRISAT's pearl millet improvement research is conducted
